ApplyLedger.note: write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3)

ApplyMultisigMutation.note: write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3); multisig threshold-gated, multi-party

ApplyMutation.note: write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3)

Backup.note: reads a consistent snapshot out to a bundle; does not mutate the live graph

BlobBegin.note: multi-call chunked-upload protocol (Begin ... ChunkPut* ... Commit); no single-call atomicity; durable via its own blob.redb (group-committed Immediate), self-routes before dispatch_graph_op

BlobChunkPut.note: durable via its own blob.redb (group-committed Immediate); self-routes before dispatch_graph_op

BlobCommit.note: multi-call chunked-upload protocol (Begin ... ChunkPut* ... Commit); no single-call atomicity; durable via its own blob.redb (group-committed Immediate), self-routes before dispatch_graph_op

BlobRef.note: refcount increment; idempotent-ish but re-invocation adds another ref, so not idempotent; durable via blob.redb

CatalogAssign.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- governed elsewhere

CatalogReassign.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- governed elsewhere

CatalogRemove.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- governed elsewhere

CausalEstimate.note: EPI-P3-3 do-calculus intervention over a request-carried SCM; handler additionally gated `epistemic-causal`

CepSubscribe.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

CepUnsubscribe.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

Checkpoint.note: flushes the ALREADY-durable WAL tail into a snapshot; does not itself add new data

ClearLedger.note: write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3)

CloseChannel.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

Commit.note: the durable-apply moment of the multi-op OCC transaction; self-routes before dispatch_graph_op

CompactNodesByType.note: write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3); recomputable

CreateChannel.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

CreateGraph.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier (it targets the registry, not an existing graph's content) -- the registry-level ACL for this is out of this workstream's scope

CreateMatView.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

CypherQuery.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) depends on a keyword scan of the query text (access::cypher_is_write); also write-but-absent-from-wal.rs's durable set (EG-P0-3)

DecaySweep.note: write per access.rs but intentionally excluded from wal.rs (recomputable maintenance op, per wal.rs doc comment)

DistributedCompute.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all; policy marks it mutates=true on semantic grounds (cross-shard Pregel writeback) -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

DropContinuousQuery.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

DropTrigger.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

EpistemicStatus.note: L53 (EPI-P3-5) acceptance capstone; handler additionally gated `epistemic-tms`

EvictLRU.note: write per access.rs but intentionally excluded from wal.rs (recomputable maintenance op, per wal.rs doc comment)

ExplainEvidence.note: CONCEPT:EG-X1 multimodal-citation resolver; handler additionally gated `evidence-graph`

ExplainProvenanceByIds.note: CONCEPT:EG-KB-CURRENCY — ID-seeded sibling of ExplainProvenance, same policy profile

Fork.note: returns the forked snapshot to the caller; never registers/persists it server-side

FromMsgpack.note: write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3); typically used for bulk restore, not incremental WAL logging

GraphLearnFit.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

GraphLearnPredict.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

GraphQl.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) depends on the parsed operation kind (query vs mutation); also write-but-absent-from-wal.rs's durable set (EG-P0-3)

IcvConfigure.note: write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3); a build without `security` drops audit entirely

ImportSqliteFile.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

JoinChannel.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

KvCas.note: durable via its own kv.redb (redb::Durability::Immediate, commit-before-ack) -- self-routes before dispatch_graph_op and before wal.rs's per-graph WAL entirely; NOT a wal.rs gap, just a parallel durability domain

KvDelete.note: durable via its own kv.redb (redb::Durability::Immediate); self-routes before dispatch_graph_op

KvPut.note: durable via its own kv.redb (redb::Durability::Immediate, commit-before-ack) -- self-routes before dispatch_graph_op and before wal.rs's per-graph WAL entirely; NOT a wal.rs gap, just a parallel durability domain

LeaveChannel.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

MaterializationStatus.note: Seam 3: read-only status lookup on the same tms_hook index; handler additionally gated `epistemic-tms`

MineAnomaly.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineAssociate.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineCausalImpact.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineClassifyFit.note: the one Mine* family member that is unconditionally read-only (produces a model blob, never writes back)

MineClassifyPredict.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineCluster.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineCommunity.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineEntityResolve.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineForecast.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound (writeback-conditional per access.rs); wal.rs::is_durable_mutation now covers the writeback=true case (EG-P0-3 fixed)

MineOntologyGap.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineProcess.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineReduce.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineRetrievalQuality.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineRiskPropagation.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineRootCause.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) returns the runtime `writeback` field

MineSequence.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound (writeback-conditional per access.rs); wal.rs::is_durable_mutation now covers the writeback=true case (EG-P0-3 fixed)

MineSubgraph.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound (writeback AND non-tfidf/non-motif algorithm-conditional per access.rs); wal.rs::is_durable_mutation now covers the gspan writeback=true case (EG-P0-3 fixed)

MineText.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound (writeback AND non-tfidf/non-motif algorithm-conditional per access.rs); wal.rs::is_durable_mutation now covers the lda/nmf writeback=true case (EG-P0-3 fixed)

MultiGraphBatchUpdate.note: spans multiple graphs in one call (not a single-graph Atomic unit); NOT present in access.rs's write classifier or wal.rs's durable set at all -- flagged as a possible coverage gap in both

ParseRepository.note: write per access.rs but intentionally excluded from wal.rs (recomputable from source tree, per wal.rs doc comment)

PlacementRoute.note: DIST-P2-4: always in the enum (pure serde); the real answer needs `raft` + a live MultiRaft cluster, else a well-formed {"explicit": false} JSON (not an error) -- see handlers/placement.rs

PlanMatViewDefine.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

PlanMatViewDrop.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

PlanMatViewRefresh.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

PruneByLifecycle.note: write per access.rs but intentionally excluded from wal.rs (recomputable maintenance op, per wal.rs doc comment)

Publish.note: PublishIdempotent is the one exception (producer-id/seq dedup makes replays idempotent by construction)

PublishConfirmed.note: PublishIdempotent is the one exception (producer-id/seq dedup makes replays idempotent by construction)

PublishEx.note: PublishIdempotent is the one exception (producer-id/seq dedup makes replays idempotent by construction)

PublishIdempotent.note: PublishIdempotent is the one exception (producer-id/seq dedup makes replays idempotent by construction)

RankByProvenance.note: EPI-P3-3 provenance-aware retrieval ranking; handler additionally gated `epistemic-causal`

RbacAdmin.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

RebalanceExecute.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- governed elsewhere

Reconcile.note: CRDT merge of remote state; write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3)

RefreshMatView.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

RegisterContinuousQuery.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

RegisterForeignSource.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all; policy marks it mutates=true on semantic grounds (registers a foreign-source config) -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

RegisterIdentity.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

RegisterMaterialization.note: Seam 3 (X-6 wire surface): registers a TruthMaintenance materialization off its own stored provenance; side effect lands only in the ephemeral tms_hook index, never the durable graph; handler additionally gated `epistemic-tms`

RegisterTrigger.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

RegisterUdf.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all; policy marks it mutates=true on semantic grounds -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

Reshard.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all (server-admin action, not a per-graph content write) -- governed by a separate admin gate, out of this workstream's scope

Restore.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all (server-admin action) -- governed elsewhere

RunDatalogReasoning.note: write per access.rs but absent from wal.rs durable set (EG-P0-3); inferred facts are recomputable

RunRules.note: READ-ONLY (EG-P0-2/L11 handler audit): handle_run_rules reasons over an off-lock analysis_snapshot and returns inferred triples, no writeback -- unlike its sibling RunDatalogReasoning which materialises in-place. Corrected from a prior mutates=true semantic guess; now agrees with access.rs (never a write there)

RunUdf.note: executes a registered sandboxed function; treated as read/compute unless the UDF itself writes back (not modeled -- the wire protocol has no writeback flag here)

SendMessage.note: NOT present in access.rs's write classifier at all -- flagged as a possible access.rs coverage gap

Shutdown.note: a server lifecycle action, not a graph-content mutation -- governed by a separate admin gate

Sql.note: mutates is a conservative upper bound: the REAL access::requires_write(m) depends on the parsed statement kind (eg_query::classify); write-but-absent-from-wal.rs's durable set too (EG-P0-3) -- SQL DML durability rides the user-table store's own persistence, not the graph WAL

TouchNodes.note: write per access.rs but intentionally excluded from wal.rs (recomputable maintenance op, per wal.rs doc comment)

TsAppend.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op, targeting its own series.redb (separate from graph.redb/blob.redb/kv.redb)

TxnAddEdge.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnAddEmbedding.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnAddMeasurement.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnAddNode.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnAxiom.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnBlobRef.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnCas.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op; see TxnAddNode note

TxnConstruct.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnMaterializeBelief.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnPlanWriteback.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op (see dispatch.rs); access::requires_write is never consulted for these -- write-access is enforced once at BeginTxn, not per-op here (out of this workstream's scope to verify)

TxnRemoveEdge.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op; see TxnAddNode note

TxnRemoveNode.note: self-routes before dispatch_graph_op; see TxnAddNode note

Watch.note: opens a push subscription; not a snapshot read nor a mutation

WhatChanged.note: L53 (EPI-P3-5) bitemporal diff; handler additionally gated `epistemic-tms`
